clusters.title: Multiple reviewers flagged sampler flag update coverage and duplicated flag-name handling

member_findings.title: Runtime sampler flag transaction has no regression test
member_findings.impact: The new UpdateFlagParameter path now batches request_sample_rate, access_sample_rate, and diagnostic_sample_rate through ValidateAndCommitSamplerFlags/CommitSamplerFlagsTransaction, including validation failure and rollback behavior. The diff only changes an unrelated flags_test assertion indentation, so a regression where gflags are committed but LogSampler is not updated, explicit derivation state is wrong, or a partial invalid batch is not rolled back would not be caught.
member_findings.fix: Add a flags unit test that drives Flags::UpdateFlagParameter with sampler flags: a valid multi-flag update that changes both FLAGS_* values and LogSampler::GetSnapshotForTest(), a request-only update that proves access/diagnostic derivation, and an invalid mixed batch that leaves prior FLAGS_* and the sampler snapshot unchanged.

member_findings.title: Sampler flag name set is duplicated across update paths
member_findings.impact: The same three sampler flag names are declared here and again in UpdateFlagParameter. Adding, renaming, or removing a sampler flag later requires multiple edits and can silently split validation from dispatch behavior if one list is missed.
member_findings.fix: Move the sampler flag names into one file-local constant or helper such as IsSamplerFlag(), and reuse it from both ValidateAndCommitSamplerFlags and UpdateFlagParameter.

clusters.title: Batch delete access logging now copies every object key

member_findings.title: Batch delete access logging now copies every object key
member_findings.impact: DeleteAllCopy previously logged ObjectKeysToAbbrStr(req.object_keys()), which only needed the first key and count. The new ObjectKeysRef(req.object_keys()) path stores a PROTO_REF, and ObjectKeyState::FillObjectKey materializes it by reserving protoRef->size() and emplacing every key into a std::vector before formatting. With the default access sample rate of 1.0, every sampled-in batch delete pays O(number of keys) heap allocations and string copies on the worker request path, even though the log output is bounded/truncated.
member_findings.fix: Keep this call on a summary path: add/use a protobuf-compatible ObjectKeysSummaryRef or ObjectKeyProvider that formats only the first key and count, and change the PROTO_REF formatter to stream directly from the repeated field instead of copying it into a vector.

clusters.title: Config snapshot retirement leaks memory across dynamic updates

member_findings.title: Config snapshot retirement leaks memory across dynamic updates
member_findings.impact: Every sampler config change exchanges in a new snapshot and appends the old one to oldSnapshots_, but production code only frees that vector during Shutdown. A long-running worker/client that receives repeated dynamic sampler updates or worker register/heartbeat config changes will retain one snapshot per change for the lifetime of the process, eventually creating unbounded memory growth and possible OOM.
member_findings.fix: Replace the raw snapshot retirement list with a safe reclamation scheme: for example publish snapshots through shared ownership/RCU epoch protection, or periodically reclaim retired snapshots after readers have quiesced, instead of keeping all old snapshots until shutdown.

clusters.title: Duplicate object-key formatting logic for protobuf keys

member_findings.title: Duplicate object-key formatting logic for protobuf keys
member_findings.impact: The new protobuf overload repeats the same truncation, count, and total-size logic as the vector overload above it. Any future change to access-log key formatting will need to be kept in sync across both implementations, which is easy to miss in this hot logging utility.
member_findings.fix: Factor the shared loop into a small helper that accepts an iterable/range of string-like keys, or have both overloads adapt their input into a common formatting function.

clusters.title: Comment relies on a brittle design-document line reference

member_findings.title: Comment relies on a brittle design-document line reference
member_findings.impact: The zero-rate branch documents its invariant as 'hard per design 533', but line-number references will drift as the design doc changes and do not explain the local reason a reject decision must not be created here. Future maintainers will have to chase external context before safely modifying this branch.
member_findings.fix: Replace the line-number reference with a short source-local rationale describing which path owns reject-decision creation/propagation and why this branch must remain read-only.
